questions.prompt: What type of substrate does a PMOS enhancement MOSFET have?
questions.explanation: A PMOS has an n-type substrate — the exact opposite of NMOS. The p⁺ source and drain regions are embedded in this n-type material. The channel that forms is p-type (holes are the carriers).

questions.prompt: What is the sign of the threshold voltage VT for a PMOS device?
options: Can be either positive or negative
questions.explanation: The threshold voltage of a PMOS is negative (typically -1 to -2 V). The turn-on condition is VGS < VT, meaning the gate must be driven sufficiently negative relative to the source.

questions.prompt: To turn ON a PMOS transistor, the gate must be:
options: More positive than the source by at least VT
More negative than the source by at least |VT|
At the same voltage as the source
questions.correctAnswer: More negative than the source by at least |VT|
questions.explanation: Since VT is negative for PMOS, VGS must be less than VT, meaning the gate must be pulled at least |VT| volts below the source. This negative field attracts holes to form the p-channel.

questions.prompt: What type of charge carriers form the channel in a PMOS device?
questions.explanation: The PMOS channel is formed by holes — minority carriers in the n-type substrate that are attracted to the surface by the negative gate voltage. The channel is p-type, hence 'P-channel MOSFET'.

questions.prompt: How does hole mobility compare to electron mobility in silicon?
questions.explanation: Hole mobility is roughly 2 to 3 times lower than electron mobility in silicon. This means PMOS transistors are inherently slower than NMOS transistors of the same size, and must be made wider to achieve the same current.

questions.prompt: In CMOS circuits, the PMOS transistor connects to:
questions.explanation: In CMOS, the PMOS forms the 'pull-up' network connected to VDD. Its source connects to VDD, so when the gate is pulled LOW (to ground), |VGS| = VDD which exceeds |VT|, turning the PMOS ON and pulling the output HIGH.

questions.prompt: In a PMOS transistor, conventional current flows from:
questions.explanation: In PMOS, holes flow from source to drain, and since holes are positive carriers, conventional current follows the same direction: source to drain. This is the opposite of NMOS, where conventional current flows drain to source.

questions.prompt: Why must PMOS transistors be physically wider than NMOS to carry the same current?
options: Because the oxide is thicker in PMOS
Because hole mobility is lower than electron mobility
Because PMOS has higher threshold voltage
questions.correctAnswer: Because hole mobility is lower than electron mobility
questions.explanation: Since Kp = μpCox'(W/L) and μp < μn, the PMOS needs a larger W/L ratio to achieve the same K value as an NMOS. Typically, PMOS is made 2–3× wider to compensate for the lower hole mobility.

questions.prompt: A PMOS has VGS = -3 V, VT = -1 V, VDS = -1 V. What region is it in?
questions.explanation: |VGS| = 3V > |VT| = 1V, so it's ON. |VDS| = 1V vs |VGS| - |VT| = 3 - 1 = 2V. Since |VDS| (1V) < |VOV| (2V), the PMOS is in the triode region.

questions.prompt: A PMOS has VGS = -3 V, VT = -1 V, VDS = -4 V. What region is it in?
questions.explanation: |VGS| = 3V > |VT| = 1V, so it's ON. |VDS| = 4V vs |VOV| = |VGS| - |VT| = 2V. Since |VDS| (4V) ≥ |VOV| (2V), the PMOS is in saturation.

questions.prompt: PMOS-only logic was the first commercial MOS technology. Why was it eventually replaced?
options: Holes are slower than electrons, making PMOS logic slower
PMOS consumed less power than alternatives
questions.correctAnswer: Holes are slower than electrons, making PMOS logic slower
questions.explanation: PMOS logic gates switch more slowly because hole mobility is lower than electron mobility. NMOS logic was faster but had static power issues. The ultimate solution was CMOS, which combines both types for speed and low power.

options: Every NMOS parameter has a mirror counterpart in PMOS with reversed polarity
PMOS is always superior to NMOS
They cannot be combined in the same circuit
questions.correctAnswer: Every NMOS parameter has a mirror counterpart in PMOS with reversed polarity
questions.explanation: The NMOS-PMOS duality means all polarities, doping types, and voltage conditions are reversed. p-substrate↔n-substrate, n⁺ S/D↔p⁺ S/D, positive VT↔negative VT, electron channel↔hole channel. This complementary nature is the foundation of CMOS technology.
